data.title: REG - GCP Infra Inv Ltd - Transaction in Own Shares
data.description:
RNS Number : 1918E GCP Infrastructure Investments Ltd 08 April 2025  

08 April 2025

GCP Infrastructure Investments Limited

(the "Company" or "GCP Infra")

LEI: 213800W64MNATSIV5Z47

Transaction in own shares

GCP Infra today announces that pursuant to the general authority granted by shareholders of the Company at the annual general meeting on 13 February 2025 to make market purchases of its own ordinary shares, it repurchased 200,000 ordinary shares at a weighted average price of 71.02 pence per share, to be held in treasury, on 08 April 2025.

Following this transaction, the Company has in issue 884,797,669 ordinary shares of which 33,152,688 are held in treasury.

For reporting purposes under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules the market should exclude any shares held in treasury and should use the figure of 851,644,981 voting rights when determining if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in the Company.

For further information, please contact:

Gravis Capital Management Limited

Philip Kent

Cameron Gardner

Max Gilbert

+44 (0)20 3405 8500

RBC Capital Markets

Matthew Coakes

Elizabeth Evans

+44 (0)20 7653 4000

Stifel Nicolaus Europe Limited

Edward Gibson-Watt

Jonathan Wilkes-Green

+44 (0)20 7710 7600

Burson Buchanan

Helen Tarbet

Samuel Adams

Henry Wilson

+44 (0)20 7466 5000

Notes to Editors

GCP Infra is a closed-ended investment company and FTSE-250 constituent whose shares are traded on the main market of the London Stock Exchange. Its objective is to provide shareholders with regular, sustained, long-term distributions and to preserve capital over the long term by generating exposure to UK infrastructure debt and related and/or similar assets.

The Company primarily targets investments in infrastructure projects with long term, public sector-backed, availability-based revenues. Where possible, investments are structured to benefit from partial inflation protection. GCP Infra is advised by Gravis Capital Management Limited.

GCP Infra has been awarded with the London Stock Exchange's Green Economy Mark in recognition of its contribution to positive environmental outcomes.

This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. data.title: US energy secretary sees tighter sanctions on Iran without deal
data.description:
Key points:
  • Absolutely would expect tighter sanctions on Iran, Wright says
  • Boosting coal production essential for AI data centers, industrial activity - Wright
  • Europeans won't want to depend on Russia for energy after Ukraine war- Wright

U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright said on Tuesday that Iran can expect tighter sanctions if it does not come to an agreement with President Donald Trump on its nuclear program.

"So absolutely, I would expect very tight sanctions on Iran, and hopefully drive them to abandon their nuclear program," Wright said in an interview with CNBC.

Wright on Wednesday will launch a nearly two-week tour of three Middle East countries, including Saudi Arabia, marking his first visit as a U.S. official to the de facto leader of the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries, a source familiar with the matter told Reuters.

Wright also defended Trump's executive order, expected later on Tuesday, to boost the coal industry as essential for artificial intelligence data centers, as well as steel production and other industrial activities.

"We need a growing supply of electricity to hit the AI boom and also for this re-industrializing of the United States. If we want to grow America's electricity production meaningfully over the next five or 10 years, we've got to stop closing coal plants," Wright said in the interview.

Asked about Trump's comment that the European Union should buy more energy from the United States, Wright said he has had countries in Asia, in Europe and elsewhere reach out to express interest in buying more American energy."

Wright said he does not think European countries will want to return to Russia for their energy supplies when the war in Ukraine ends.

"As I talk to European leaders, one thing they all share is a regret that they bet their energy future on Russia," he said. "So I don't think there's a huge desire right now that, when the war is over, we're going to we're going to re-count on Russia for a dominant share of our energy supply. I think that's very unlikely to unfold."

data.title: Rice Futures at 2021-Lows
data.description:
Rice futures have been trading around $13.60 per hundredweight, maintaining levels close to the November 2021 lows, as US President Donald Trump’s move to impose import tariffs weigh on the demand outlook while supplies remain plentiful.

Nations exporting rice to the U.S. were hit hard by new tariffs announced on April 2, including Thailand, the top supplier, facing a 36% tariff.

Other major exporters like India (26%), Vietnam (46%), and Pakistan (29%) also saw duties imposed.

The U.S. produces about 80% of its rice, according to the USA Rice Federation, and is the world’s fifth-largest rice exporter, with most production coming from the Southeast, Gulf Coast, and West Coast. Meanwhile, supply remains strong in top producer India, with surplus stocks and favorable crops, as African buyers have shown limited demand.

data.title: Nomura Slashes Its Canada GDP Growth Forecasts for 2025, 2026; Ups 2025 Inflation View
data.description:
United States President Donald Trump's aggressive trade actions pose severe downside risks to economic growth in

Canada, said Nomura.

The bank revised down its growth forecasts for both this year and 2026, and expects a contraction in Q2 2025, owing to a sharp decline in Canadian exports, a tick down in fixed investment and a pullback in consumer spending.

Nomura predicts Canada's GDP to grow 1.2% this year from an earlier estimate of 2.2%, while it forecasts 2026 GDP to expand 1.4% from a prior view of 2.3%

Business investment has remained subdued, and the latest survey data suggest firms plan to curtail investment and postpone hiring in light of recent trade uncertainty. Consumers'

perception of financial health has deteriorated, while spending plans have worsened. In addition, labor market slack and moderating labor income are headwinds for consumption in the near term.

A potential fiscal relief announcement following federal elections at the end of April could somewhat soften the blow from U.S. tariffs, added Nomura.

Since Canada's economy is closely tethered to the U.S., the bank expects a pick-up in inflation in 2025. Weak demand and softening rent inflation are likely to offset an increase in consumer prices due to tariffs in the medium term.

Nomura sees inflation remaining within the Bank of Canada's

target range of 1%-3% in 2025-26. Rising inflation expectations and upside risks to goods prices are likely to limit large cuts. The bank now sees the consumer price index rising to 2.4% this year from the 2.0% predicted earlier. It still forecasts CPI at 1.8% in 2026.

Risks of a recession have increased significantly, according to the bank. However, a modest expansion remains Nomura's baseline scenario. It maintains its long-held call for the BoC to ease in April and conclude its cutting cycle. Risks are skewed towards more cuts if tariffs lead to a sharp growth downturn.

data.title: The CAC 40 Index Closes 2.50% Higher
data.description:
In Paris, the CAC 40 Index gained 173 points or 2.50 percent on Tuesday.

Gains were led by Cap Gemini (5.11%), Thales (4.83%) and Hermes International (4.78%).

data.title: Western Michigan University To Offer $159.4M in Bonds for Student Housing
data.description:
By Patrick Sheridan

The Board of Trustees of Western Michigan University will issue $159.4 million in bonds to build a new residence hall.

The Series 2025 A General Revenue Bonds have maturities ranging from 2025 to 2045. Two term bonds will also be offered with maturities of 2049 and 2054 according to the preliminary official statement posted on MuniOS on Monday.

Pricing is slated for April 16 with a closing date of May 15.

Interest on the bonds is payable on each May 17 and Nov. 15.

The bonds are limited obligations of the Board of Trustees, payable solely from a pledge of general revenues of the Board.

Proceeds of the bond sale will be used to construct a new residence hall for first-year students. The Valley housing project will offer a variety of single and double-occupancy rooms, and is planned to open for the fall 2026 semester. Upon completion of the facility, the University expects to operate five residence halls with a total capacity of 4,265 students.

Founded in 1903 as a teacher's college, Western Michigan University is located in Kalamazoo, Michigan and is divided into five campuses. Total student enrollment for Fall 2024 was 17,605 with 79% of the student body composed of undergraduates, and 21% graduate students.

For fiscal 2024 the university generated total revenue of $626.3 million, an increase of 15.1% from fiscal 2020, according to the roadshow document accompanying the preliminary official statement.

S&P Global Ratings and Moody's Ratings have assigned A and Aa3 ratings to the bonds, respectively.

Barclays is the senior managing underwriter on the offering. BofA Securities is the co-managing underwriter.

Write to Patrick Sheridan at patrick.sheridan@wsj.com

data.title: The DAX Index Closes 2.48% Higher
data.description:
In Frankfurt, the DAX Index rose 491 points or 2.48 percent on Tuesday.

Leading the gains are Commerzbank (6.27%), HeidelbergCement (6.20%) and Sartorius (5.52%).

data.title: REG - Howden Joinery Grp - Transaction in Own Shares
data.description:
RNS Number : 1919E Howden Joinery Group PLC 08 April 2025  

HOWDEN JOINERY GROUP PLC

(the "Company")

TRANSACTION IN OWN SHARES

The Company announces that on 8 April 2025 it purchased through Numis Securities Limited ("Deutsche Numis"), the following number of its ordinary shares of 10p each ("Shares") for cancellation at an average price of 692.80 pence per Share:

Number of Shares purchased and subsequently cancelled:

50,000

Highest purchase price paid per Share:

699.00p

Lowest purchase price paid per Share:

682.00p

Following the purchase of the Shares, the Company holds 3,844,331 Shares in treasury and 548,797,389 Shares in issue (excluding those held as treasury shares). This figure (548,797,389) represents the total voting rights in the Company and may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they can determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

This announcement is made in accordance with the requirements of LR 9.6.6.

Enquiries should be directed to:

Forbes McNaughton

Company Secretary

020 7535 1110

Company Legal Entity Identifier: 213800KC1LJOJTY3JP45

Transaction details

In accordance with Article 5(1)(b) of Regulation (EU) No.596/2014 as it applies in the UK (Market Abuse Regulation), a full breakdown of the individual trades made by Deutsche Numis on behalf of the Company as part of the buyback programme is attached to this announcement.

data.title: Market Chatter: TSMC May Face Fine Topping $1 Billion to Settle US Chip Probe
data.description:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing may face a penalty of $1 billion or more to settle a US Commerce Department investigation into a chip it made that was discovered in an AI processor from China's Huawei, Reuters reported Tuesday, citing people familiar with the matter.

TSMC didn't immediately reply to a request for comment from MT Newswires.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Codelco aims for upper end of copper output range, plans return to bond market, CEO says
data.description:
The CEO of Chile's Codelco, Ruben Alvarado, said on Tuesday that the state-run copper producer aims to bring output to the upper end of its estimated range this year, expecting to reach 1.39 million metric tons.

Alvarado said the world's largest copper producer plans to return to the bond market but is evaluating the timing.

"We know we still have to go to market. We'll certainly be there, but we still have to decide when," he told Reuters on the sidelines at the CRU/CESCO World Copper Conference.

"Our bond prices are very attractive. We take great care with our investment grade, so we believe we still have the room to move forward with financing our projects."

In 2024, Codelco managed to boost production after hitting 25-year lows in 2023 due to delays in key projects and operational issues, which it has been resolving.

Alvarado said he expects to increase performance after having a better first quarter than last year.

"This year, we're preparing to go for significantly more," he said, adding the expectation is 1.39 million tons.

"We're focused on doing things right, ensuring operational continuity and being particularly careful with safety issues," he added.

Alvarado also noted that the company is still evaluating whether to send new spot copper shipments to the U.S., as it did in the first quarter when customers were trying to get ahead of expected U.S. tariffs on imports.

"What has motivated us to increase shipments to the United States has to do primarily with meeting the needs of our long-term customers," he said.

data.title: Wells Fargo Adjusts Price Target on Trane Technologies to $330 From $360, Maintains Underweight Rating
data.description: Trane Technologies has an average rating of hold and mean price target of $407.02, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Swiss Stocks Join Regional Rally in Europe
data.description:
Swiss stocks recovered on Tuesday, joining a regional rally of European blue-chip indices after four days of losses driven by mounting US tariff concerns.

The Swiss Market Index closed 2.82% higher amid a quiet day of local economic news.

In corporate news, Swiss electricity supplier Romande Energie Holding booked a year-over-year drop in 2024 net profit to 26.1 million francs from the restated 150.4 million francs, dented by regulatory pressures and falling energy supply margin. Net revenue fell 10% to 832.3 million francs, with adjusted EBITDA also down over the period. Looking forward, the group expects adjusted EBITDA and adjusted EBIT for 2025 to be in line with 2024 levels, while EBITDA is projected to grow 50% by 2030. Shares were up 0.92% at closing.

"The muted guidance does not indicate a significant recovery. Furthermore, the low return levels raise the question if Romande Energie should really pursue its significant expansion strategy from a financial perspective. The group targets an EBITDA increase of 50% to 2030, however, without a return ambition it is difficult to judge on the attractiveness for investors," analysts at Baader Helvea said. "A weak results momentum with an ongoing management transition and a strategy with low financial visibility makes it difficult to turn more positive on Romande Energie in the short-term."

Meanwhile, Lonza Group gained 7.62% after Chief Financial Officer Philippe Deecke reassured analysts in a call that the Swiss pharmaceutical manufacturer is "well protected" from potential US tariffs on the pharma sector due to its business model and global infrastructure, RBC Capital Markets said.

data.title: Foreign investors' Russian state bond purchases in March highest since mid-2019, central bank says
data.description:
Foreign investors from "friendly" countries bought 25 billion roubles ($291.22 million) worth of Russian state bonds in March, the highest volume since the middle of 2019, Russia's central bank said on Tuesday.

It also said state bond yields had fallen at the start of the month thanks to easing geopolitical tensions but had begun rising after the central bank kept the key interest rate on hold at 21%.

The central bank said foreign investors had also bought Russian companies' shares worth 13.4 billion roubles in March.

($1 = 85.8455 roubles)

data.title: Review of financial reporting - Grieg Seafood ASA
data.description:
Finanstilsynet has reviewed an accounting matter in the interim financialreportof Grieg Seafood ASA for the 4th quarter of 2024. Finanstilsynet has concludedthat the interim financial report contains errors and omissions relating todisclosure requirements in IAS 36 Impairment of assets no. 124-137. Withoutsuchdisclosures, Finanstilsynet considers that users of the entity's interimreportfor the 4th quarter of 2024 are unable to understand the drivers and magnitudeof the impairment that significantly changed the entity's financial positionfrom the end of the previous annual reporting period, cf. IAS 34 InterimFinancial Reporting No. 15 and 15A.

If you have any questions, please contact:

Lars Jacob Braarud, Head of section, +47 22 93 97 53 / +47 91 34 52 73(mobile)

https://newsweb.oslobors.no/message/643244
